behavior: Local known models are derived from ModelIdSchema so the drift check uses the same model universe as routing policy.
The OpenAI model catalog parser accepts the official list response shape with model id, object, created, and owned_by fields.
The fetch wrapper calls /models with Authorization supplied from an injected apiKey or OPENAI_API_KEY.
The drift detector reports availableKnownModels, missingKnownModels, untrackedOfficialModels, ignoredOfficialModelCount, and warnings.
The default relevance filter tracks GPT-5 and Codex model ids while ignoring unrelated model families such as embedding, audio, or image models.

coveredScenarios.name: current_official_coverage
coveredScenarios.result: status current when every local policy model appears in the supplied official catalog

coveredScenarios.name: official_addition_and_removal
coveredScenarios.result: status drift_detected when a known model disappears and new relevant official models are not yet modeled

coveredScenarios.name: official_shape_and_fetch_wrapper
coveredScenarios.result: parser and injected fetch wrapper handle the official list response without live network access

limitations: No live OpenAI /models request was executed for this evidence file.
No API key or Authorization header value is recorded.
This adds library-level detection only; a user-facing CLI command or scheduled check can be wired in a later step.
